blast_radius: data plane CONTROL+reward (policy now drives tech/policy; schema VERSION 2) + 1-line NextTurnAutomation.adoptPolicy guard + new desktop/core policy classes + new python/unciv_train package + 6 tests; ConsoleLauncher/SimBenchmark behavior unchanged (flag-gated).
council: REQUEST_CHANGES → revised (control wiring added; 1 fundamental gap + refinements folded; no KILL/BLOCK)

risks.text: Control wiring incorrect — policy doesn't truly drive tech/policy ⇒ meaningless ~50% curve (FND-0002)
risks.mitigation: D14 control seam (pre-fill techsToResearch + adopt + adoptPolicy guard); test asserts applied tech == policy's chosen tech; recorded==applied by single-chooseIndex design
single concat(global,acting_civ) path; golden PARITY test @1e-4 from a committed fixture; widths runtime-derived from schema.json
VERSION 2 + explicit recordTerminal; reward-extraction + no-negative-index gather tests; determinism guards bytes
ThreadLocal memo keyed by (gameId,civID,turn); concurrent-eval smoke test
accepted for v1 stationary-opponent go/no-go; map seed varies; baseline; optional γ; documented

human_actions: Optional: pip install -e python/ for training deps (torch/onnx/onnxruntime)
rollback: Revert the branch (master untouched); schema rollback = VERSION back to 1 + regenerate (datasets perishable by design).

change_inventory.why: D14 control (drive tech/policy) split from emit; recordTerminal — per-civ terminal ±1
D14 guard: adoptPolicy skips heuristic for policy-controlled civs
score-leader-at-cap flag; call recordTerminal; use SimStats
public binomialTest + scoreLeader (extracted, reused by EVAL)
in-JVM ONNX inference, masked sample/argmax, per-turn memo
onnxruntime impl dep + selfPlay JavaExec task
pin onnxruntime version in the catalog
contract, model, dataset, train, export_onnx, run_loop

verification: pytest python/tests/test_parity.py — JVM vs Python logits within 1e-4 (AC4)
python -m unciv_train.run_loop --rounds 10 — produces curve.csv + curve.png (AC1, AC5)
out_of_scope: full entity/spatial net input (v1 = global+acting_civ only)
open_questions: Exact GnK policyCount (70 vs ~80) — resolved empirically in build step 1 (read schema.json); contract reads it at runtime regardless
